title: rottboellia corymbosa subvar. pubescens
intro: Rottboellia corymbosa subvar. pubescens, also known as the hairy panic grass, is a plant species in the Poaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rottboellia corymbosa subvar. pubescens include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin infections, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
